description: §6.6.10 — a V5-strict derived cell whose initializer reads its own value (`const <counter> = @counter + @seed`) is the degenerate 1-cycle self-reference. This fires E-DERIVED-CIRCULAR-DEP as a fatal error and blocks codegen. Regression guard for Rx-1: the V5-strict `const <x> = expr` form (structuralForm:true) was previously routed through the plain-state collector, so its self-referencing dep was never detected and the self-cycle compiled clean in the real pipeline.
rationale: §6.6.10 line 2712: a derived cell cannot depend on its own value — the degenerate 1-cycle self-reference is forbidden because it forms an infinite recompute loop. The V5-strict `const <x> = expr` form is a derived cell and must be scanned for self-reference exactly like the legacy `@`-form. E-DERIVED-CIRCULAR-DEP is fatal (§34), so the self-cyclic derived cell never reaches codegen output.
